Operation 'SetRideTypes' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is vague about the action being performed. data.current_summary: Preset types of rides for sandbox
data.current_description: The sandbox-ridetypes endpoint allows you to preset the ridetypes in the region surrounding the specified latitude and longitude to allow testing different scenarios
data.summary_suggestion: Configure available ride types for sandbox testing
data.description_suggestion: Configures the available ride types in the sandbox environment for a specified geographic region (latitude and longitude). This operation allows testing different ride type scenarios by presetting which RideTypeEnum values are available in the target region. Accepts a SandboxRideType request object and returns the configured ride types. This is a sandbox-only operation for testing purposes and does not affect production data.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the action being performed. Description lacks clarity on inputs (latitude/longitude parameters), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (sandbox-only, testing context). Added specificity about RideTypeEnum usage, request/response schemas, and emphasized sandbox-only nature for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'SetRideTypeAvailability' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original summary conflates driver availability with ride request processing, which is unclear. Description lacks specificity about the ride_type parameter, request/response details, and sandbox context. Added clarity on path parameter usage, request schema reference, return value, and emphasized sandbox-only nature. Improved actionability by clearly stating what configuration is being set.

data.current_summary: Driver availability for processing ride request
data.current_description: Set driver availability for the provided ride_type in the city/region surrounding the specified location
data.summary_suggestion: Configure driver availability for ride type in sandbox
data.description_suggestion: Configures driver availability for a specific ride type in the sandbox environment for the geographic region surrounding a specified location. The ride_type path parameter identifies which RideTypeEnum to configure. Accepts a SandboxDriverAvailability request object to set availability parameters. Returns the updated driver availability configuration. This sandbox-only operation enables testing different driver availability scenarios without affecting production systems.
data.reasoning: Original summary conflates driver availability with ride request processing, which is unclear. Description lacks specificity about the ride_type parameter, request/response details, and sandbox context. Added clarity on path parameter usage, request schema reference, return value, and emphasized sandbox-only nature. Improved actionability by clearly stating what configuration is being set.

data.current_description: Estimate the cost of taking a Lyft between two points.
data.summary_suggestion: Estimate Lyft ride cost between locations
data.description_suggestion: Estimates the cost of a Lyft ride between two specified locations. Requires pickup and dropoff coordinates as input parameters. Returns a Cost object containing the estimated fare breakdown, including base fare, distance charges, and time charges. Useful for displaying price information to users before ride request.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks details about required inputs (coordinates), what the Cost response contains, and the use case. Enhanced version clarifies parameters, response structure, and AI agent decision-making context.

data.current_description: The drivers endpoint returns a list of nearby drivers' lat and lng at a given location.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of Lyft drivers currently available near a specified location. Requires location coordinates (latitude/longitude) as input parameters. Returns driver location data including their current lat/lng coordinates. This endpoint helps determine driver availability and proximity before requesting a ride.

data.current_description: The ETA endpoint lets you know how quickly a Lyft driver can come get you
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the estimated time of arrival (ETA) for a Lyft driver to reach a specified pickup location. Requires location coordinates and optionally ride type as input parameters. Returns ETA data indicating the estimated minutes until driver arrival. Helps users understand wait times before confirming a ride request.
data.reasoning: Summary is too vague and informal. Description lacks specificity about required inputs, response format, and practical use. Enhanced version clarifies what parameters are needed, what is returned, and why an AI agent would use this operation.

data.current_description: The v1 of this endpoint returns the user's ID, v2 will return more general info about the user. We require authentication for this endpoint, so we extract the user ID from the access token.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the authenticated user's profile information including user ID and general account details. Requires User Authentication via OAuth 2.0. Returns a user profile object extracted from the authenticated access token. This endpoint requires an authenticated user context and does not accept path parameters.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description contains implementation details (version history, token extraction) that are not relevant to API consumers and confuses the operation's purpose. data.current_description: Get a list of past & current rides for this passenger.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of past and current rides for the authenticated passenger. Supports filtering and pagination via optional parameters (limit, offset, etc.). Returns an array of RideDetail objects containing information about each ride including status, driver details, vehicle information, and pricing. Requires User Authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description lacks information about available parameters for filtering/pagination and the structure of returned data. Enhanced version clarifies input options, response format, and authentication requirements for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Get the ride detail of a given ride ID
data.current_description: Get the status of a ride along with information about the driver, vehicle and price of a given ride ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a specific ride using its ride ID. Requires the ride ID as a path parameter. Returns a RideDetail object containing the ride status, driver information (name, rating, vehicle details), vehicle information, pricing breakdown, and pickup/dropoff locations. Requires User Authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is awkwardly phrased and redundant. Description lacks clarity about the required path parameter and doesn't specify the complete response structure. message: Operation 'CancelRide' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary has grammatical issues ('a ongoing' should be 'an active'). Description lacks clarity about what is returned, potential side effects (cancellation fees), and state requirements. data.current_description: Cancel a ongoing ride which was requested earlier by providing the ride id.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active ride that was previously requested. Requires the ride ID as a path parameter and a cancellation request body. Returns the updated ride details with canceled status. Note: Cancellation may incur a penalty fee depending on the ride's current status and timing. The ride must be in an active state (not already dropped off or canceled).
data.reasoning: Original summary has grammatical issues ('a ongoing' should be 'an active'). Description lacks clarity about what is returned, potential side effects (cancellation fees), and state requirements. Added details about request body, response format, cancellation penalties, and state constraints for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'SetRideDestination' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify the request format (Location object), response format (RideDetail), and clarify that destination changes may impact cost/duration estimates, which is important for AI agents to understand the operation's side effects.

data.current_summary: Update the destination of the ride
data.current_description: Add or update the ride's destination. Note that the ride must still be active (not droppedOff or canceled), and that destinations on Lyft Line rides can not be changed.
data.description_suggestion: Adds or updates the destination for an active ride. Accepts a Location object specifying the new destination. Returns the updated RideDetail object reflecting the new destination. Constraints: the ride must be in an active state (not dropped off or canceled), and destination changes are not permitted for Lyft Line rides. This operation may affect the ride's estimated cost and duration.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify the request format (Location object), response format (RideDetail), and clarify that destination changes may impact cost/duration estimates, which is important for AI agents to understand the operation's side effects.

data.current_summary: Get the receipt of the rides.
data.current_description: Get the receipt information of a processed ride by providing the ride id. Receipts will only be available to view once the payment has been processed. In the case of canceled ride, cancellation penalty is included if applicable.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve the receipt for a completed ride
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the receipt information for a processed ride using the ride ID. Returns a receipt object containing itemized charges, payment details, and any applicable fees. Receipts are only available after payment processing is complete. For canceled rides, the receipt includes any applicable cancellation penalties. This endpoint requires the ride to have completed payment processing.
data.reasoning: Original summary is grammatically awkward ('of the rides' should be 'for a ride'). Description lacks clarity about response format and structure. Enhanced summary to be more precise and description to specify return format (receipt object with itemized charges), clarify availability conditions, and improve AI agent understanding of when this operation can be successfully used.

data.current_description: The ride types endpoint returns information about what kinds of Lyft rides you can request at a given location.
data.summary_suggestion: Get available ride types at a location
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the available ride types that can be requested at a specified location. Accepts location parameters (latitude, longitude) and optionally ride type filters. Returns a list of RideTypeEnum objects with details about each available ride type including pricing information, estimated wait times, and ride characteristics. This endpoint helps determine which ride options are available before requesting a ride.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and not action-oriented. Description lacks specificity about input parameters and response format. message: Operation 'SetPrimeTime' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary lacks context about sandbox purpose. Enhanced to specify request schema (SandboxPrimetime), clarify sandbox-specific behavior, explain the side effect (affects all subsequent cost estimates and ride requests), and improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Preset a Prime Time percentage in the region surrounding the specified location. This Prime Time percentage will be applied when requesting cost, or when requesting a ride in sandbox mode.
data.summary_suggestion: Set Prime Time percentage for sandbox testing
data.description_suggestion: Sets a Prime Time percentage for the region surrounding a specified location in sandbox mode. Accepts a SandboxPrimetime request object containing the location and desired Prime Time percentage. This preset percentage will be applied to all cost estimates and ride requests in the sandbox environment for that region. Returns confirmation of the updated Prime Time setting. This operation is used for testing pricing scenarios in sandbox mode.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks context about sandbox purpose. Description is vague about request/response formats and doesn't clarify that this is a sandbox-only testing operation. Enhanced to specify request schema (SandboxPrimetime), clarify sandbox-specific behavior, explain the side effect (affects all subsequent cost estimates and ride requests), and improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

Returns the updated RideDetail object reflecting the new status. Valid status transitions follow the ride lifecycle (e.g., requested → accepted → arrived → in_progress → dropped_off). This operation is used for testing ride workflows and state transitions in sandbox mode without requiring actual driver or passenger interactions.
